retrieved_docs.title: Mass Spectrometer -- Quality Control and Lab Safety
retrieved_docs.content: When working in the laboratory, it is crucial to exercise caution while preparing samples before introducing the compound into the mass spectrometer for analysis. For example, using the proper measuring tools for accuracy during chromatography is essential. [63] If quality control is not used, resultant peaks may form in the mass spectrum, which does not correspond to ions within the sample. [64]

retrieved_docs.title: Mass Spectrometer -- Interfering Factors
retrieved_docs.content: The primary sources of interference in mass spectrometry typically occur during sample preparation and the measurement of separated ions within the spectrometer. Interference can occur due to inaccurate sample handling when acquiring from patients or improper quality control. [42] Interference alters the concentration of the molecules, which can result in the inaccuracy of the mass spectrometer graph due to potential contaminants or other ions appearing. [43]

retrieved_docs.title: Mass Spectrometer -- Testing Procedures
retrieved_docs.content: In mass spectrometry, several key components are involved in analyzing a sample.

retrieved_docs.title: Chromatography -- Clinical Significance -- Diabetes Mellitus and Other Metabolic Disorders
retrieved_docs.content: The hemoglobin A1c (HbA1c) is one of the gold standard tests for diagnosing diabetes mellitus and is measured using high-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC). Among the National Glycohemoglobin Standardization Program (NGSP) recommended methods for HbA1c, HPLC offers the least interference, high accuracy, and traceability. In the pediatric age group, it is used to quantify different steroids and aids in the diagnosis of congenital adrenal hyperplasia, hyperaldosteronism, Cushing's disease, and Addison's disease. LC-MS is more accurate than conventional immunoassay for diagnosing the above conditions. [36]

retrieved_docs.title: Chromatography -- Interfering Factors
retrieved_docs.content: When similar drug molecules or metabolites are tested, the antibodies may bind to these related substances in the patient sample in a  non-specific manner, causing erroneous results. The structural similarities among steroid hormones, including differences in keto groups, double bonds, and OH groups, can lead to interference in affinity chromatography analysis, particularly when hormones with high serum concentrations (eg, cortisol) interfere with hormones of lower concentration but similar structures (eg,11-deoxycortisol). Similarly, high concentrations of dihydroxy epiandrosterone sulfate can interfere with estradiol, 17-OH progesterone, and testosterone. Furthermore, steroid metabolites contribute to the spectrum of potential interfering molecules. [30]

retrieved_docs.title: Chromatography -- Quality Control and Lab Safety
retrieved_docs.content: Analytical accuracy is typically assessed through reference materials, internal reference samples, and participation in proficiency testing to ensure quality performance. Whether the sample is urine or blood, an internal standard or a substance similar to the substance of interest (analyte) must be set in the GC-MS. For example, if morphine is the analyte, nalorphine may be used as an internal standard. [43] Regular calibration of instruments, proper sample handling, and meticulous record-keeping are essential to maintain precision. Chromatography involves the usage of potentially caustic skin irritants such as ethanol or NaOH. Care must be taken in advance to ensure minimal exposure to biological substances and irritation. [44]

retrieved_docs.title: Chromatography -- Interfering Factors
retrieved_docs.content: Interfering factors during different stages of chromatography can affect the assay. Contaminants in the patient sample or during the sample preparation step might introduce unwanted contents and affect the detection. The presence of moisture or air bubbles in the samples might alter the retention time. Sample degradation after preparation and exposure to light, improper storage, handling, or extraction can alter the composition of analytes, affecting separation. [26] Overloading a GC machine with too much sample volume can cause the mixture to separate poorly. The column must be allowed to fully elute its contents before injecting the following sample.

retrieved_docs.title: 21-Hydroxylase Deficiency -- Evaluation -- Diagnostic Approach to 21-Hydroxylase Deficiency and Associated Tests
retrieved_docs.content: Newborn screening programs routinely test for 21-hydroxylase deficiency. Levels of 17-hydroxyprogesterone are very high (cutoff of >1000 ng/dL, but frequently >5000-10,000) in patients with the classic form. [8] Although liquid chromatography-tandem mass spectrometry (LC-MS/MS) is the current standard of care, immunoassays are still used in many parts of the world and are a source of false-positive results. Therefore, either second-tier LC-MS/MS or cosyntropin stimulation testing should be performed before initiating steroids in cases of mild elevation of 17-hydroxyprogesterone. Other than this, if 17-hydroxyprogesterone levels are ambiguous (200-1000 ng/dL) or other enzyme defects, such as 11-beta-hydroxylase, 3-beta-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase, or p450 oxidoreductase deficiency, are suspected, cosyntropin stimulation testing should be performed. This test evaluates the complete adrenal profile, including measurements of 17-hydroxyprogesterone, cortisol, 11-deoxycorticosterone, 11-deoxycortisol, 17-hydroxypregnenolone, dehydroepiandrosterone, and androstenedione. Samples can be obtained immediately before and 60 minutes after administering 0.25 mg of cosyntropin, a synthetic ACTH. Cosyntropin administration provides a pharmacologic stimulus to the adrenal glands, maximizing hormone secretion. A stimulation test is typically unnecessary to diagnose a typical 21-hydroxylase deficiency CAH with a concordant clinical profile and unambiguously high 17-hydroxyprogesterone.

retrieved_docs.title: 24-Hour Urine Collection and Analysis -- Quality Control and Lab Safety
retrieved_docs.content: Nomograms for creatinine excretion based on age, weight, sex, and sometimes ethnicity have been developed to compare expected creatinine levels in 24-hour samples, helping to assess sample validity. [10] [19] [21] If the sample differs by more than 30% from the expected value, it likely indicates over-collection, under-collection, or the presence of an interfering factor. [10] While expected creatinine values vary, a commonly used threshold is that less than 15 mg/kg for women and less than 20 mg/kg for men in a 24-hour collection suggests under-collection. Another validation method compares expected creatinine values with muscle mass measured by bioimpedance, as muscle mass strongly correlates with creatinine levels. However, this test is not commonly performed. [21] One formula used to calculate the expected 24-hour urine creatinine, as derived by Gerber et al, is as follows:

retrieved_docs.title: Dexamethasone Suppression Test -- Quality Control and Lab Safety
retrieved_docs.content: For nonwaived tests, laboratory regulations require, at the minimum, analysis of at least 2 levels of control materials once every 24 hours. If necessary, laboratories can assay quality control (QC) samples more frequently to ensure accurate results. QC samples should be assayed after calibration or maintenance of an analyzer to verify the correct method performance. [53] To minimize QC when performing tests for which manufacturers' recommendations are less than those required by the regulatory agency, such as once per month, the laboratories can develop an individualized quality control plan (IQCP) that involves performing a risk assessment of potential sources of error in all phases of testing and putting in place a QC plan to reduce the likelihood of errors. [54]

retrieved_docs.title: Mass Spectrometer -- Testing Procedures -- Ionization
retrieved_docs.content: Unlike electron ionization in GC/MS, most liquid chromatography/mass spectrometry (LC/MS) ionization techniques are conducted at atmospheric pressure. Electrospray ionization and atmospheric pressure chemical ionization are soft ionization techniques that leave the molecular ion largely intact in the source. [27] Many LC/MS techniques use technologies after the source, in the mass analyzer, to fragment molecules and generate the daughter or fragment ions used in identification. [28] However, ionization techniques in LC/MS produce fragments and, therefore, mass spectra that are somewhat less reproducible between instruments compared to the electron ionization method used in gas GC/MS. [29]

retrieved_docs.title: Polycystic Ovarian Syndrome -- Evaluation -- Hyperandrogenism Diagnostic Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: Laboratories should prioritize using liquid chromatography or mass spectrometry assays over direct immunoassays (eg, radiometric and enzyme-linked assays) for assessing total or free testosterone, as direct immunoassays have limited accuracy and exhibit poor sensitivity and precision for diagnosing hyperandrogenism in PCOS. If androgen levels are significantly above laboratory reference ranges, healthcare professionals should consider other causes of hyperandrogenemia, including ovarian and adrenal neoplasms, congenital adrenal hyperplasia, Cushing's syndrome, ovarian hyperthecosis (particularly after menopause), iatrogenic effects, and syndromes associated with severe insulin resistance. However, some androgen-secreting neoplasms may only cause mild to moderate increases in androgen levels. Therefore, taking a clinical history that includes the timing of symptom onset and any rapid progression of symptoms is crucial for evaluating the possibility of an androgen-secreting tumor. [30]

retrieved_docs.content: The allowed qualifier is used to denote hormones that inhibit the release of pituitary hormones, and this inhibitory action is recognized as an effect of drugs.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2065
retrieved_docs.content: Androgens and female sex hormones in combination with other drugs are classified as a specific type of androgens and female sex hormones in combination.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 3 -- Community 1435
retrieved_docs.content: Androgens and estrogens are classified as members of the broader hormone group that includes estrogens and testosterone, reflecting their role as sex steroids.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1503
retrieved_docs.content: Acyclovir, akamprosát, albuminy, botulotoxiny, chlorid draselný, chromonar, dideoxyinosin, ochranné látky, promethazin, sacharát železitý, thyreoglobulin, clonidin, desonid, ergotamin, estetrol, fenfluramin, klorazepát dvojdraselný, kombinace amlodipin / valsartan a kyselina chenodeoxycholová jsou uznány jako povolené kvalifikátory pro analytické postupy v klinické laboratoři, což umožňuje jejich detekci, kvantifikaci a monitorování v biologických vzorcích a podporuje komplexní farmakologické sledování, toxikologické screenování a diagnostické hodnocení pacientů. Většina těchto látek má etablované terapeutické indikace: acyclovir jako antivirotikum, akamprosát jako antidiaréické činidlo, albuminy jako plazmatické expandéry, botulotoxiny v neurologických onemocněních, chlorid draselný jako elektrolytický doplněk, chromonar jako kontrastní látka, dideoxyinosin jako nukleosidový analog, ochranné látky v různých farmakologických formulacích, promethazin jako antihistaminikum, sacharát železitý jako železný suplement, thyreoglobulin jako štítní protein využívaný při monitorování nebo léčebných hodnoceních, clonidin a kombinace amlodipin / valsartan jako antihypertenziva, desonid jako antineoplastikum, ergotamin jako vazokonstriktor, estetrol jako estrogen, fenfluramin jako anorektikum, klorazepát dvojdraselný jako benzodiazepin a kyselina chenodeoxycholová jako žlučová kyselina. Tamsulosin a promethazin jsou rovněž uznány jako léky povolené pro terapeutické použití, což potvrzuje jejich vhodnost k předepisování při příslušných klinických indikacích. Spirosloučeniny a tamsulosin jsou identifikovány jako látky s toxickým potenciálem, což vyžaduje zvýšenou opatrnost při manipulaci a podávání a jejich zahrnutí do seznamu možných otrav je klíčové pro rychlé klinické hodnocení, diagnózu a léčbu. Rozpoznání těchto látek jak jako terapeutických prostředků, tak jako potenciálních toxinů, je zásadní pro bezpečnou péči o pacienta a pro přesné laboratorní vyšetření.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 3 -- Community 1808
retrieved_docs.content: matrix capable of containing many of these qualifiers, allowing their measurement, detection, or monitoring in clinical and toxicological contexts; a specific link between spiro‑compounds and urinary testing is noted. All listed substances possess potential adverse effect profiles, underscoring the importance of safety evaluation, risk assessment, patient counseling, and therapeutic drug monitoring when prescribing, administering, or interpreting laboratory results. Collectively, the allowed qualifier framework supports drug development, diagnostic imaging, assessment of environmental or lifestyle exposures, and risk management in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 4 -- Community 2738
retrieved_docs.content: The allowed qualifier denotes a deficiency of síra (sulfur) and also a deficiency of testosteron (testosterone). This indicates that when the allowed qualifier is applied, it signals insufficient levels of both sulfur, an essential element involved in various metabolic pathways, and testosterone, a key hormone regulating many physiological functions. Recognizing and addressing these deficiencies is important for maintaining metabolic health and hormonal balance in patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 4 -- Community 2933
retrieved_docs.content: Androgeny and the class of compounds known as androgens are both classified as forms of nandrolone, a synthetic anabolic‑androgenic steroid. As variants of nandrolone, they share the characteristic hormonal activity of this compound, which includes promoting protein synthesis, increasing muscle mass, and exerting androgenic effects. Recognizing androgeny and androgens as types of nandrolone helps clarify their pharmacologic profile and informs clinical decisions regarding their therapeutic use, potential side‑effects, and regulatory considerations.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 3 -- Community 2279
retrieved_docs.content: Luteinizing Hormone‑releasing Hormone Agonist is classified as a hormone and related agent.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 4 -- Level 4 -- Community 2803
retrieved_docs.content: Androgens are a class of steroid hormones that serve as male sex hormones, and nandrolone is a specific androgenic anabolic steroid within this group, known for its therapeutic use in conditions requiring anabolic effects.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 12 -- Level 4 -- Community 2744
retrieved_docs.content: The allowed qualifier “nedostatek” (deficiency) can be applied to several specific substances, indicating that a lack of each is recognized as a distinct medical condition. Inosine deficiency, nitrous‑oxide (oxid dusnatý) deficiency, sulfur (síra) deficiency, testosterone deficiency, and theobromin deficiency are all valid qualifiers under this framework. Recognizing these deficiencies is important for clinical assessment, as each missing compound may contribute to particular physiological disturbances and may require targeted evaluation and treatment to restore normal levels.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 19 -- Level 1 -- Community 226
retrieved_docs.content: adrenaline, testosterone propionate, trisaccharides and vasotocin, which can act as substrates, intermediates or products, underscoring the regulatory complexity of metabolism and its relevance for therapeutic and research applications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2534
retrieved_docs.content: ANABOLIC AGENTS FOR SYSTEMIC USE are classified as synthetic androgens, which are man‑made compounds that mimic the biological activity of natural male sex hormones such as testosterone. As synthetic androgens, these agents are administered systemically to produce anabolic effects—including enhanced protein synthesis, muscle growth, and bone density—and are employed in therapeutic settings such as hormone‑deficient states, certain anemias, and muscle‑wasting disorders. Their synthetic nature also confers potential androgenic side effects, necessitating careful medical oversight during use.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 4 -- Community 2848
retrieved_docs.content: Prostaglandin antagonists can be described in terms of a deficiency, and within this framework both a deficiency of sulfur and a deficiency of testosterone are recognized as permissible qualifiers for characterizing prostaglandin antagonist activity.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 4 -- Community 2763
retrieved_docs.content: The allowed qualifier is applicable to both androgeny and chemie. In the context of chemie, it denotes inhibitory activity against 5‑alpha‑reductase as well as inhibition of Na‑K‑Cl symporters. This relationship highlights that the allowed qualifier can be used to describe androgen‑related effects and specific chemical actions that block the enzyme 5‑alpha‑reductase and the Na‑K‑Cl transport system.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 7 -- Level 4 -- Community 2803
retrieved_docs.content: Estradiol is classified as a hormone, representing a specific instance within the broader category of hormones.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 4 -- Community 2782
retrieved_docs.content: The concept of an “Allowed qualifier” is linked to several specific deficiencies. It is associated with a deficiency of inosine, indicating that the allowed qualifier denotes the presence of insufficient inosine. In addition, the allowed qualifier is connected to a deficiency of sulfur (síra), signifying that this qualifier also marks inadequate sulfur levels. Finally, the allowed qualifier is related to a deficiency of testosterone, showing that it similarly identifies insufficient testosterone. Together, these relationships suggest that the allowed qualifier serves as a marker for recognized deficiencies in inosine, sulfur, and testosterone, which can be important considerations in clinical assessment and patient care.
